summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted .jervis.yml with a malicious matrix exclude value breaks out of the single-quoted string literal in the Groovy expression generated by matrixExcludeFilterBuilder() and executes arbitrary Groovy code via GroovyShell.evaluate() in MultiPlatformGenerator.getBuildableMatrixAxes().
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the crafted matrix exclude payload to inject a Groovy file-write statement that creates /tmp/evohunt_proof with the exact content GROOVY_CODE_INJECTION_SUCCESSFUL, demonstrating arbitrary code execution on the host running the GroovyShell.evaluate() call.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Jervis library from the target repository, loaded the malicious .jervis.yml through the full MultiPlatformGenerator pipeline, and the injected Groovy code created the proof file with the expected content, confirming arbitrary code execution via GroovyShell.evaluate().
verification_method: The exploit script checks that /tmp/evohunt_proof exists and contains exactly the string GROOVY_CODE_INJECTION_SUCCESSFUL. This distinguishes real code injection from a parser error, startup failure, or unrelated crash because the proof string can only be written by the injected Groovy code executing inside the GroovyShell.evaluate() call.
evidence_produced_by_run: The proof file /tmp/evohunt_proof was created with content GROOVY_CODE_INJECTION_SUCCESSFUL, confirming the injected new File(...).text = ... statement executed.
The matrixExcludeFilter() output logged before evaluation shows the unsanitized injection payload embedded in the Groovy expression.
The Gradle build and exploit execution completed with exit code 0.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
